title: Gold, the Dollar, and the Oval Office
excerpt: A quantitative test of whether presidential pressure on the Fed amplifies gold's sensitivity to a weakening dollar. Combines historical review of Nixon, Greenspan, and Trump with a regression model showing DXY beta jumps 37% during political pressure regimes (p < 0.0001). Key finding: gold returns roughly double to 21%/yr during pressure periods, and 95% of the excess is captured by standard macro drivers.

title: Rate Cuts and Their Implications for Crypto, Vol. 2
excerpt: Rate cuts with inflation above 3% — is the Fed pivoting on economics, or on $39 trillion of national debt pressure? Argues the real calculus is political, and that a fragile Iran ceasefire leaves energy prices as a wildcard that could derail the AI-deflation narrative underpinning the dovish case.

title: Hyperscalers或将让出基础云业务给 DePIN
excerpt: 七巨头控制全球~25–30%的数据中心容量，增长压力正迫使其将资源转向高利润的AI业务，基础云计算反而成为包袱。在经济放缓背景下，近50%企业仅使用基础云服务，价格与可靠性优势可能为DePIN的广泛采用打开结构性窗口。

title: Hyperscalers May Cede Basic Cloud Business to DePIN
excerpt: AWS, Google Cloud, and Azure control 70% of the public cloud market — but growth pressure is forcing them upmarket into AI, leaving basic cloud services vulnerable to price hikes and degradation. Argues this creates a structural opening for DePIN networks to compete on cost and resilience, right as nearly half of enterprises use only basic cloud.

title: Duration of This Bubble Burst and Future Outlook
excerpt: Pushes past the liquidity narrative to identify deeper structural drivers of the crypto downturn: two decades of QE without long-term solutions, bipartisan dysfunction dampening US productivity, and AI's capital demands compounding the debt problem. Until these root causes ease — not just tariffs — the recovery thesis remains fragile.

title: The Rise of the Machine Economy: peaq ($PEAQ) in 2026
excerpt: By 2030, ~1 billion humanoid robots could exist — and peaq is building the infrastructure for machines to hold wallets, price services, and settle transactions on-chain. Maps the thesis to a robotics market projected to grow from $109B to $376B by 2034 at 15% CAGR.

title: Bitcoin's Role in a Fractured Macro and Its Risks
excerpt: A deliberately cautious counterpoint: while markets are bundling BTC with gold as 'safety,' Bitcoin's store-of-value claim is still aspirational — a decade of adoption vs. millennia, concentrated ownership, US-centric mining, and CBDC competition. In the next stress regime, BTC may not behave the way the narrative promises.

title: Rate Cuts and Their Implications for Crypto
excerpt: Ahead of a widely anticipated rate cut, argues the move is politically influenced — driven more by Treasury funding pressure and Trump administration demands than independent Fed analysis. Warns that stacked cuts could flood stablecoins with liquidity, accelerating a bubble-and-bust dynamic while inflation risk goes underpriced.

title: Market Outlook Under the New US-CN Tariff Deal
excerpt: The US-China tariff deal offers short-term relief but masks the real story: US urgency was driven by the looming X-date debt ceiling risk and the need to stabilize Treasury yields. With sticky inflation and tiered trade realignments ahead, crypto and gold gain as hedges against continued dollar weakness.

title: ETH's Recent Recovery Is Backed by Solid Foundation, Not Speculation
excerpt: Pushes back on the narrative that ETH's price surge is purely speculative — triangulates across macro (dollar pressure driving demand for digital store-of-value), sector (tightening correlation with large caps), and protocol (ongoing network upgrades creating structural demand) to argue the foundation beneath this rally is genuine.

title: Bitcoin Nears the Brink of Rapid Growth
excerpt: Makes the bull case for Bitcoin entering an accelerated growth phase in 2025: declining global trade, a looming US recession, and eroding dollar confidence remain intact despite calming official rhetoric. Flags the risk that a tiered trade negotiation strategy could create geopolitical friction if nations in later rounds reject less favorable terms.
